content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates some perceptual tracking beyond pure content — notably in turn 4, where they notice the linguistic signature of 'things are easy at home' coming out unprompted and reflect that back ('was that in response to a question I asked, or did that just come out first?'). This is a genuine read of the happiness-filter showing up reflexively. However, after that single moment, the coach does not name any further reads despite multiple visible cues: the small smile (turn 5), the small laugh and the comparison-to-sister deflection (turn 7), the head tilt with persistent smile (turn 11), and crucially the longer pause and topic-shift away from career territory (turn 13). The coach asks good follow-up questions but they are not grounded in named observations — they are blind questions. The 'why not?' in turn 14 follows a clear bracing/compartmentalizing moment that the coach could have named ('I notice you just separated that off as not relationship territory') but didn't. One specific read early, then content-tracking inquiry — that's a 2, not a 3.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Topic-level question; no read named."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names a specific linguistic read — the unprompted nature of 'things are easy at home' — and inquires into it. This is an AoA-quality read of the happiness filter surfacing reflexively."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Hypothetical question; doesn't ground in a read of what's happening in the client right now."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Good inquiry but blind — doesn't name the smile, the comparison-deflection, or the defensive framing in turn 7."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Picks up content thread ('not push on things') but misses naming the head tilt / persistent smile / managing tone."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Useful diagnostic-adjacent question but again not grounded in an observation."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client clearly compartmentalizes ('not really relationship territory') after a longer pause — a visible bracing/sorting move. Coach asks 'why not?' without naming what they just saw, missing a chance to make the read operative."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["the-happiness-filter"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["walking-on-eggshells", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "notes": "One real read early establishes capability; absence of further reads through clear somatic/linguistic cues prevents a 3."
}
